Examples.Title: Acknowledge Work
Examples.Description:
Note: these endpoints are called automatically by the pre-built environment worker provided in the SDKs and CLI, for orchestrating sessions with self-hosted sandbox environments. They are included here as a reference; you do not need to invoke them directly.

Acknowledge receipt of a work item, transitioning it from 'queued' to 'starting' and removing it from the queue.
Examples.Code:
### Acknowledge Work
# @name beta_acknowledge_work_v1_environments__environment_id__work__work_id__ack_post
POST {{host}}/v1/environments/{{environment_id}}/work/{{work_id}}/ack?beta=true
anthropic-beta: {{anthropic-beta}}
anthropic-version: {{anthropic-version}}
authorization: {{authorization}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Successful Response
# Content-Type: application/json
# 4XX
# Description: Error response.

See our [errors documentation](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/errors) for more details.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Create Deployment
Examples.Code:
### Create Deployment
# @name BetaCreateDeployment
POST {{host}}/v1/deployments?beta=true
anthropic-version: {{anthropic-version}}
anthropic-beta: {{anthropic-beta}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "name": "string",
  "description": "string",
  "agent": {},
  "environment_id": "string",
  "vault_ids": [
    "string"
  ],
  "initial_events": [
    {
      "type": "user.message",
      "content": [
        {
          "type": "text",
          "text": "Where is my order #1234?"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "resources": [
    {
      "type": "file",
      "file_id": "file_011CNha8iCJcU1wXNR6q4V8w",
      "mount_path": "/uploads/receipt.pdf"
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {},
  "schedule": {}
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Successful response (OK)
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Invalid argument - The client specified an invalid argument
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Unauthenticated - The request does not have valid authentication credentials
# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: Permission denied - The caller does not have permission to execute the specified operation
# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: Not found - Some requested entity was not found
# Content-Type: application/json
# 408
# Description: Deadline exceeded - The deadline expired before the operation could complete
# Content-Type: application/json
# 409
# Description: Aborted - The operation was aborted due to concurrency issue
# Content-Type: application/json
# 412
# Description: Failed precondition - Operation was rejected because the system is not in required state
# Content-Type: application/json
# 413
# Description: Out of range - Operation was attempted past the valid range
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Resource exhausted - Some resource has been exhausted (rate limiting)
# Content-Type: application/json
# 431
# Description: Request header fields too large - Request metadata was too large
# Content-Type: application/json
# 499
# Description: Cancelled - The operation was cancelled by the client
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Internal - Internal server error
# Content-Type: application/json
# 501
# Description: Unimplemented - The operation is not implemented or supported
# Content-Type: application/json
# 503
# Description: Unavailable - The service is currently unavailable
# Content-Type: application/json
# 504
# Description: Deadline exceeded - Upstream service did not respond in time
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Get Work Item
Examples.Description:
Note: these endpoints are called automatically by the pre-built environment worker provided in the SDKs and CLI, for orchestrating sessions with self-hosted sandbox environments. They are included here as a reference; you do not need to invoke them directly.

Retrieve detailed information about a specific work item.
Examples.Code:
### Get Work Item
# @name beta_get_work_v1_environments__environment_id__work__work_id__get
GET {{host}}/v1/environments/{{environment_id}}/work/{{work_id}}?beta=true
anthropic-beta: {{anthropic-beta}}
anthropic-version: {{anthropic-version}}
x-api-key: {{x-api-key}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Successful Response
# Content-Type: application/json
# 4XX
# Description: Error response.

See our [errors documentation](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/errors) for more details.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Create a Message
Examples.Description:
Send a structured list of input messages with text and/or image content, and the model will generate the next message in the conversation.

The Messages API can be used for either single queries or stateless multi-turn conversations.

Learn more about the Messages API in our [user guide](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/get-started)
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Anthropic;` is in scope.

Examples.Title: Record Heartbeat
Examples.Description:
Note: these endpoints are called automatically by the pre-built environment worker provided in the SDKs and CLI, for orchestrating sessions with self-hosted sandbox environments. They are included here as a reference; you do not need to invoke them directly.

Record a heartbeat for a work item to maintain the lease.
Examples.Code:
### Record Heartbeat
# @name beta_record_heartbeat_v1_environments__environment_id__work__work_id__heartbeat_post
POST {{host}}/v1/environments/{{environment_id}}/work/{{work_id}}/heartbeat?beta=true?desired_ttl_seconds={{desired_ttl_seconds}}&expected_last_heartbeat=NO_HEARTBEAT
anthropic-beta: {{anthropic-beta}}
anthropic-version: {{anthropic-version}}
authorization: {{authorization}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Successful Response
# Content-Type: application/json
# 4XX
# Description: Error response.

See our [errors documentation](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/errors) for more details.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Stop Work
Examples.Description:
Note: these endpoints are called automatically by the pre-built environment worker provided in the SDKs and CLI, for orchestrating sessions with self-hosted sandbox environments. They are included here as a reference; you do not need to invoke them directly.

Stop a work item, initiating graceful or forced shutdown.
Examples.Code:
### Stop Work
# @name beta_stop_work_v1_environments__environment_id__work__work_id__stop_post
POST {{host}}/v1/environments/{{environment_id}}/work/{{work_id}}/stop?beta=true
anthropic-beta: {{anthropic-beta}}
anthropic-version: {{anthropic-version}}
authorization: {{authorization}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "force": false
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Successful Response
# Content-Type: application/json
# 4XX
# Description: Error response.

See our [errors documentation](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/errors) for more details.
# Content-Type: application/json

Note: these endpoints are called automatically by the pre-built environment worker provided in the SDKs and CLI, for orchestrating sessions with self-hosted sandbox environments. They are included here as a reference; you do not need to invoke them directly.

Update work item metadata with merge semantics.

# @name beta_update_work_v1_environments__environment_id__work__work_id__post
POST {{host}}/v1/environments/{{environment_id}}/work/{{work_id}}?beta=true
anthropic-beta: {{anthropic-beta}}
anthropic-version: {{anthropic-version}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "metadata": {}
}

Examples.Title: Create a Text Completion
Examples.Description:
[Legacy] Create a Text Completion.

The Text Completions API is a legacy API. We recommend using the [Messages API](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/messages) going forward.

Future models and features will not be compatible with Text Completions. See our [migration guide](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/working-with-messages) for guidance in migrating from Text Completions to Messages.
Examples.Code:
### Create a Text Completion
# @name complete_post
POST {{host}}/v1/complete
anthropic-version: {{anthropic-version}}
anthropic-beta: {{anthropic-beta}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "model": "string",
  "prompt": "string",
  "max_tokens_to_sample": 0,
  "stop_sequences": [
    "string"
  ],
  "temperature": 0,
  "top_p": 0,
  "top_k": 0,
  "metadata": {
    "user_id": "string"
  },
  "stream": true
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Text Completion object.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 4XX
# Description: Error response.

See our [errors documentation](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/errors) for more details.
# Content-Type: application/json
